content.problemStatement:
There are NNN Pokemon trainers numbered from 111 to NNN. Each trainer has **one** Pokemon. All the trainers have arrived to participate in a contest.

There are two battle arenas, one in ground and other in water. Each Pokemon may have different power in both the arenas.

When two Pokemon fight in an arena, the Pokemon having higher power in that arena wins. It is guaranteed that all Pokemon have **distinct** powers in the same arena to avoid any ties.

The strength of a Pokemon trainer is determined to be the number of other Pokemon his Pokemon can defeat in **at least one** arena.

It is known that only the Pokemon trainers with the **highest** strength will qualify for the next round (multiple trainers may have the same strength). Find the number of Pokemon trainers who will qualify for the next round.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of Pokemon trainers.
The second line will contain NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_{1},A_{2},\ldots,A_{N}A1​,A2​,…,AN​, where AiA_{i}Ai​ denotes the power of the Pokemon of ithi^{th}ith trainer in the ground arena.
The third line will contain NNN space-separated integers, B1,B2,…,BNB_{1},B_{2},\ldots,B_{N}B1​,B2​,…,BN​, where BiB_{i}Bi​ denotes the power of the Pokemon of ithi^{th}ith trainer in the water arena.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number Pokemon trainers who will qualify for the next round.
constraints: The elements of array AAA are distinct.
The elements of array BBB are distinct.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^{5}2⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: The second trainer's Pokemon can defeat the first trainer's Pokemon in both arenas so his strength is 111. Similarly, the first trainer's Pokemon can not defeat the second trainer's Pokemon in any arena so his strength will be 000. Hence, only the second trainer will qualify.
Test case 2: The second trainer's Pokemon can defeat the first trainer's Pokemon in ground arena so his strength is 111 and the first trainer's Pokemon can defeat the second trainer's Pokemon in water arena so his strength will also be 111. Hence both of them will qualify.
Test case 4: The maximum strength possible is 444. There are 333 trainers with strength 444:

Trainer 333: Trainer 333's pokemon has ground power equal to 555, so, it can defeat all other 444 pokemons in the ground arena. Thus, the trainer's strength is 444.
Trainer 444: Trainer 444's pokemon has ground power equal to 444. It can beat the pokemons having ground power less than 444. These belong to trainers 1,2,1, 2,1,2, and 555. Similarly, it has water power equal to 555. Thus, it can also beat trainer 333's pokemon which has water power 111. Thus, total strength of this trainer is 444.
Trainer 555: Trainer 555's pokemon has water power equal to 666, so, it can defeat all other 444 pokemons in the water arena. Thus, the trainer's strength is 444.
